query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "openrath: session-centered runtime state for agent systems" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it investigates the same core mechanism (reusing prior computational states/plans to optimize LLM agent performance) and measures the same dependent variable (latency reduction) within the same domain (LLM-driven agent systems), providing essential prior work for a study extending session-centered runtime state management.
